Go Fundamentals.aiRubric: Check Go syntax, variables, arrays, slices, maps, structs, and error handling.

lessons.title: Variables, Types & Syntax
lessons.theory:
## Go Variables
Go uses strong static typing with inference via `:=`.
```go
package main
import "fmt"

func main() {
    var name string = "Ada"
    age := 25
    fmt.Println(name, age)
}
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Profile Setup
Declare variables for a user: `username` (string), `level` (int), `score` (float64), and `isActive` (bool). Use the `:=` short variable declaration. Print them.
lessons.starterCode:
package main
import "fmt"

func main() {
    username ___ "___"
    level ___ ___
    score ___ ___
    isActive ___ ___

    fmt.Println(username, level, score, isActive)
}
lessons.solution:
package main
import "fmt"
func main() {
    username := "Ada"
    level := 1
    score := 100.5
    isActive := true
    fmt.Println(username, level, score, isActive)
}

lessons.title: Functions & Control Structures
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Multiply Function
Write a function `multiply` that takes two `int` parameters and returns their product as an `int`. Call it and print the result.
lessons.hint: func multiply(a int, b int) int
lessons.rubric: Function signature and return type correct.

lessons.title: Arrays, Slices & Maps
lessons.theory:
## Slices and Maps
Slices are dynamic arrays. Maps are key-value stores.
```go
nums := []int{1, 2, 3}
scores := make(map[string]int)
scores["Ada"] = 95
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Map Usage
Create a map mapping string names to int scores. Add 'Ada' -> 95, and print the map.
lessons.starterCode:
package main
import "fmt"

func main() {
    scores := make(___[___]___)
    scores["Ada"] = ___
    fmt.Println(scores)
}
lessons.solution:
package main
import "fmt"
func main() {
    scores := make(map[string]int)
    scores["Ada"] = 95
    fmt.Println(scores)
}

lessons.title: Structs & Methods
lessons.instructions:
## Task: User Struct
Define a `User` struct with a `Name` string field. Write a `Greet` method for it.
lessons.hint: type User struct. Receiver is (u User).

lessons.title: Pointers & Error Handling
lessons.theory:
## Pointers and Errors
Go uses explicit error handling, often returning `error` as the last return value.
```go
func doWork() (int, error) { return 0, errors.New("Fail") }
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Error Check
Call a function `divide(10, 0)` that returns an int and an error. If the error is not `nil`, print the error.

lessons.title: Core Concepts of Go Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Understanding Go Fundamentals\nBefore diving into complex implementations, it's essential to understand the foundational principles of Go Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Define Core Concept\n1. Create a function `get_concept()` that returns the string 'Go Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.hint: Return the exact string 'Go Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.rubric: Function returns the correct concept string.

lessons.title: Setting up the Environment for Go Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Tooling\nProperly configuring your development environment is the first step in mastering Go Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment Status\n1. Create a function `is_ready()` that returns True.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax and Operations
lessons.theory: ## Syntax Rules\nEvery discipline has its syntax. Here we apply standard operations relevant to Go Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Basic Math\n1. Return the sum of a and b.
lessons.starterCode: def add(a, b):\n    return a ___ b
lessons.solution: def add(a, b):\n    return a + b

lessons.title: Control Structures in Go Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Branching\nConditional logic dictates how applications respond to different states.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Check State\n1. Return 'Active' if state is 1, else 'Inactive'.

lessons.title: Advanced Memory Management
lessons.theory: ## Resource Allocation\nEfficient memory use is critical, especially when applying Go Fundamentals at scale.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clear Memory\n1. Set the variable `cache` to an empty dictionary.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Approaches
lessons.theory: ## OOP\nEncapsulating state and behavior within objects helps structure Go Fundamentals projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Create Class\n1. Create a class `Module` with a property `name` initialized to 'Go Fundamentals'.

lessons.title: Functional Programming Paradigms
lessons.theory: ## Pure Functions\nFunctional programming avoids side effects. This is increasingly popular in Go Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pure Function\n1. Write a function `square(x)` that returns x squared.

lessons.title: Error Handling and Debugging
lessons.theory: ## Resilience\nHandling exceptions gracefully ensures robustness in Go Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Try/Except\n1. Wrap the division in a try block, catch ZeroDivisionError, and return None.

lessons.title: Advanced Debugging in Go Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Debugging at Scale\nFinding hard-to-reproduce bugs requires structured methodology.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Debug Setup\n1. Return True for debugging active.

lessons.title: Profiling Go Fundamentals Applications
lessons.theory: ## Performance Profiling\nIdentifying bottlenecks is the first step in optimization.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Profile Scope\n1. Set `profile_mode` to 'High'.

lessons.title: Memory Leaks and Management
lessons.theory: ## Garbage Collection\nUnderstanding how memory is freed is critical.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: GC Status\n1. Set `gc_enabled` to True.

lessons.title: Multi-threading Constructs
lessons.theory: ## Parallelism\nUtilizing multiple cores improves execution speed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Thread Count\n1. Set `threads` to 8.

lessons.title: Final Project: Go Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Capstone\nApply all learned concepts to build a comprehensive solution.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Project Initialization\n1. Set `project_name` to 'Go Fundamentals Final'.

Advanced Go & Concurrency.aiRubric: Check interface implementations, goroutines, channels, mutexes, and context usage.

lessons.title: Interfaces & Type Assertions
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Shape Interface
Define an `Area()` method for a `Circle` struct that satisfies a `Shape` interface.

lessons.title: Goroutines (Concurrency Basics)
lessons.theory:
## Goroutines
Lightweight threads managed by the Go runtime.
```go
go func() {
    fmt.Println("Running asynchronously")
}()
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Simple Goroutine
Launch a goroutine that prints 'Hello' and sleep the main thread for 1 second.
lessons.rubric: go keyword used to launch routine.

lessons.title: Channels & Select Statements
lessons.theory:
## Channels
Channels are the pipes that connect concurrent goroutines.
```go
ch := make(chan int)
go func() { ch <- 42 }()
val := <-ch
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Send to Channel
Create a channel of ints. Launch a goroutine that sends the number `10` into it. Receive and print it in main.

lessons.title: Mutexes & WaitGroups
lessons.theory:
## WaitGroups
`sync.WaitGroup` waits for a collection of goroutines to finish.
```go
var wg sync.WaitGroup
wg.Add(1)
go func() { defer wg.Done(); }()
wg.Wait()
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: WaitGroup Setup
Use a WaitGroup to wait for one goroutine to finish.

lessons.title: The Context Package
lessons.theory:
## Context
Used for deadlines, cancellation, and passing request-scoped values.
```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
cancel()
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Cancel Context
Create a cancellable context from `context.Background()`, defer its cancellation, and check if it's done.

lessons.title: Concurrency and Async Operations
lessons.theory: ## Asynchronous Work\nBlocking operations freeze applications. Async allows overlapping tasks in Advanced Go & Concurrency.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Async Function\n1. Define an async function `fetch_data()`.

lessons.title: Working with Data Structures
lessons.theory: ## Data Organization\nChoosing the right data structure dictates performance.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Use a Set\n1. Return a set containing the elements 1, 2, 3.

lessons.title: Network Communication Protocols
lessons.theory: ## Connectivity\nUnderstanding HTTP/TCP is essential for networked components in Advanced Go & Concurrency.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: HTTP Status\n1. Return 200 for OK.

lessons.title: File I/O and Storage
lessons.theory: ## Persistence\nSaving data to disk allows state to survive reboots.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Open File\n1. Write code to open 'data.txt' in read mode.

lessons.title: Security Best Practices
lessons.theory: ## Defense\nProtecting applications from vulnerabilities is non-negotiable.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Hash Length\n1. Return 64, the length of a SHA-256 hash in hex.

lessons.title: Performance Optimization Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Efficiency\nOptimizing critical paths improves throughput in Advanced Go & Concurrency.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Time Complexity\n1. Return 'O(1)' for constant time complexity.

lessons.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Validation\nUnit tests verify that individual components work as expected.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Assert True\n1. Write an assert statement that 1 equals 1.

lessons.title: Integration Testing
lessons.theory: ## System Harmony\nIntegration tests ensure that components function together.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Integration Status\n1. Set `integrated` to True.

Cloud DevOps & Microservices.aiRubric: Check HTTP routing, JSON marshaling, DB logic, Docker knowledge, and tests.

lessons.title: Building REST APIs with net/http
lessons.theory:
## net/http
The standard library contains a powerful HTTP server.
```go
http.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {})
http.ListenAndServe(":8080", nil)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Simple Server
Write a handler that writes 'Hello API' to the response.

lessons.title: JSON, Serialization & Middleware
lessons.theory:
## JSON
Use `encoding/json` to marshal/unmarshal structs.
```go
type User struct { Name string `json:"name"` }
data, _ := json.Marshal(User{"Ada"})
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: JSON Marshal
Marshal a struct into JSON bytes and print them as a string.

lessons.title: Database Connections (database/sql)
lessons.theory:
## database/sql
Standard interface for SQL databases.
```go
db, err := sql.Open("postgres", dsn)
rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id FROM users")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Query Execution
Write the `db.Query` syntax to select all rows from 'users'.

lessons.title: Dockerizing Go Applications
lessons.theory:
## Docker + Go
Multi-stage builds are common to produce small binaries.
```dockerfile
FROM golang:1.20 as builder
RUN go build -o app .
FROM scratch
COPY --from=builder /app /app
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Dockerfile CMD
Write a Dockerfile snippet that sets the working directory and defines the run command.
lessons.solution:
FROM alpine:latest
WORKDIR /app
COPY myapp .
CMD ["./myapp"]

lessons.title: Testing & Benchmarking in Go
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Write a Test
Write a test function `TestSubtract` that uses `t.Errorf` if `subtract(5, 3)` is not `2`.

lessons.title: Continuous Integration Setup
lessons.theory: ## CI Pipelines\nCI automatically runs tests when code is pushed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pipeline Step\n1. Create a list with 'build', 'test', 'deploy'.

lessons.title: Deployment Pipelines
lessons.theory: ## Shipping Code\nDeploying reliably requires automated release pipelines.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment\n1. Set `env` to 'production'.

lessons.theory: ## Observability\nLogs help diagnose issues in production.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Log Level\n1. Set `level` to 'ERROR'.

lessons.title: Design Patterns in Cloud DevOps & Microservices

lessons.instructions: ## Task: Singleton\n1. Set `pattern` to 'Singleton'.

lessons.title: API Integration Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Connecting Services\nAPIs allow different systems to communicate.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: JSON Data\n1. Set `format` to 'JSON'.

lessons.title: Database Connectivity
